title: Hydrogen Peroxide Standard Measurement Protocol
author: Peter Havel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ypqfvmw
materials: for a typical standard curve. Table

steps.title: H 2 O 2 Standard Wells...
steps.instruction: H 2 O 2 Standard Wells - add 20 µl of standard (tubes A-G) and 10 µl of HPLC-grade water per well in the designated wells on the plate (see Sample Plate Format, Figure 1, page 7). Figure 1. Sample plate format

steps.title: Sample Wells - Each sample should...
steps.instruction: Sample Wells - Each sample should have at least two wells that will not contain catalase and two wells that will contain catalase. Add 20 µl of sample to the sample and sample + catalase wells. Then add 10 µl of catalase to the catalase wells and 10 µl of HPLC-grade water to the non-catalase wells

steps.title: Add 200 µl of Working Reagent to each well
steps.instruction: Add 200 µl of Working Reagent to each well. Cover the plate with the plate cover and incubate on a shaker for one hour at room temperature.

steps.title: Remove the plate cover and read...
steps.instruction: Remove the plate cover and read the absorbance at 595 nm using a plate reader.

steps.title: Calculate the average absorbance of each...
steps.instruction: Calculate the average absorbance of each standard, sample, and sample + catalase.

steps.title: Substract the average absorbance of standard...
steps.instruction: Substract the average absorbance of standard A from itself and from all other standards and samples including the catalase containing samples.

steps.title: Plot the corrected absorbance of standards...
steps.instruction: Plot the corrected absorbance of standards (from step 2 above) as a function of the final H 2 O 2 concentration (µM) from Table

steps.title: Subtract the catalase sample absorbance from...
steps.instruction: Subtract the catalase sample absorbance from the non-catalase sample absprbance to yield the corrected sample absorbance.

steps.title: Calculate the H 2 O 2...
steps.instruction: Calculate the H 2 O 2 concentration of the samples using the equation obtained from the linear regression of the standard curve substituting corrected absorbance values for each sample.
